vitals.participation_trend: growing, part of international series with 60,000 annual participants

course_description.overview: Gran Fondo road cycling event through the picturesque rolling hills, historic landscapes, and Apennine foothills of Parma's food country, with routes including 133 km main distance.

key_segments.name: Main Granfondo Loop
key_segments.description: Rolling hills with 2800m elevation gain through nature, forests, and gastronomic region.

key_segments.name: Shorter Route
key_segments.description: Alternative route option through similar hilly terrain.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging gran fondo with significant elevation in the Apennine foothills, suitable for experienced amateur cyclists seeking a Tour de France-style experience on closed roads.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Parma Airport or nearby Bologna/Milan airports; event based at Parco Ducale in Parma.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and stays in Parma, known for food and tourism; book early for event weekend.
logistics.registration_details: Registrations open via official L'Étape Series website; multiple distances available.
logistics.bike_logistics: Bike rental and transport options through Tour de France partners; roads closed to traffic.
logistics.local_considerations: Parma food valley with gastronomy focus; expect party atmosphere with side events.

final_verdict.one_liner: Tour de France-branded gran fondo in Parma's stunning food hills.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Amateur cyclists seeking pro-style road event with Italian charm and climbs.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the prestige, scenery, and gastronomy in a growing international series.
pro_tips: Choose 133km for full challenge with 2800m climbing.
Combine with Parma food experiences post-race.
Register early for L'Étape Series perks.

videos.title: Highlights | 🇮🇹 L'Etape Parma by Tour de France | 2025 🚲 🍕
videos.channel: L'Etape Series by Tour de France
videos.description:
---------

L'Etape Series is a series of amateur cycling events designed to experience the Tour de France Legend. It is the only event where you can race and feel like a Tour de France champion, and where you can enjoy the Tour de France unique atmosphere and passion.

“The Tour de France is the world's greatest cycling race. Each year it is passionately followed by fans around the world so it has naturally given rise to L'Etape by Tour de France series”, says Christian Prudhomme, Tour de France director. 

The series has now expanded to more than 22 international locations including:

🇫🇷 France
🇧🇷 Brazil
🇧🇬 Bulgaria
🇨🇷 Costa Rica
🇨🇿 Czech Republic
🇩🇰 Denmark
🇪🇨 Ecuador
🇺🇸 United States
🇬🇷 Greece
🇦🇪 United Arab Emirates
🇮🇪 Ireland
🇮🇹 Italy
🇲🇽 Mexico
🇵🇱 Poland
🇷🇴 Romania
🇸🇰 Slovakia
🇸🇮 Slovenia
🇹🇼 Taiwan
🇹🇷 Turkey


Among these Tour de France official cycling events is of course L'Étape du Tour. With a peloton of 45 000 riders and 50 nationalities, it is Europe's biggest cycling event. Riding on the same routes and under the same conditions as Tour de France champions: this is the promise that L'Étape du Tour has kept since 1993.

Check out https://www.letapebytourdefrance.com/ for more information.

Join us on:
-Facebook 👉 https://www.facebook.com/letapeseries/
-Instagram 👉 https://www.instagram.com/letapeseries/
-Strava 👉 https://www.strava.com/clubs/145034/

videos.title: Highlights Granfondo L'Etape Parma 2025
videos.description:
L'Étape Parma by Tour de France è l'evento ufficiale del Tour de France per cicloturisti e cicloamatori che si è svolto sabato 3 e domenica 4 maggio 2025 al Parco Ducale di Parma, portando in città l’atmosfera della Grande Boucle.

Il momento clou è stato la Granfondo e la vittoria assoluta nel percorso lungo è andata a Marco Rinaldi (Pigarella Multisport), di Parma, già piazzato nel 2024 nella granfondo di casa. Erano due i possibili percorsi: il lungo di 134 km per 2.660 metri di dislivello e il corto di 81,1 km per 1.095 metri di dislivello. L’evento, co-organizzato da Extragiro e Comune di Parma in collaborazione con A.S.O. e la Regione Emilia-Romagna, ha visto partecipanti da 20 Paesi che hanno formato una lunga fila di ciclisti al momento del via dal parco Ducale che è proseguita per oltre 5 minuti, dopo il via dato con Giammaria Manghi, Coordinatore delle Politiche Sportive della Regione Emilia-Romagna.

Tutti hanno potuto sfidarsi per conquistare le maglie Gialla, A Pois, Verde e Bianca del Tour de France, respirando così ancora di più la leggenda del Tour de France.
